rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical-polar crossover (RPC) coupling of ortho-vinyl benzamides with C-H acidic malonate-type esters to assemble multi-ester functionalized benzoxazines and iminoisobenzofurans.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation: base (tBuOK) deprotonates the C-H acidic ester to a stabilized carbanion, which is oxidized at the graphite anode to an electrophilic C-centered radical; after radical addition to the alkene, the resulting benzylic radical is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic carbocation (RPC). Cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical adduct from radical addition to o-vinyl benzamide
benzylic carbocation after second anodic oxidation (RPC)

electron_flow: tBuOK deprotonates the malonate ester; the carbanion is oxidized at the anode to an electrophilic radical, which adds to the styrenyl alkene of the o-vinyl benzamide to give a benzylic radical; this radical is oxidized again at the anode to a benzylic cation (radical-polar crossover), which is trapped intramolecularly by the amide oxygen (after amide tautomerization) to give the O-heterocycle, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of substrate 1 (o-vinyl benzamide)
probe_reagents.observation: No oxidation observed between 0 and 1.30 V vs SCE, indicating alkene 1 is not directly oxidized.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of substrate 2 (C-H acidic ester)
probe_reagents.observation: Shows oxidation in the accessible window; anodic oxidation of the deprotonated C-H nucleophile is strongly preferred, supporting the RPC pathway with 2 as the species oxidized at the anode.

notes: Mechanism is supported primarily by CV comparison of the two substrates plus literature precedent; no radical trap (TEMPO/BHT), KIE, or DFT experiments are reported on these pages. The amide acts as a tethered O-nucleophile, with amide isomerization (imidic acid tautomer) invoked to enable O-attack on the benzylic cation; depending on the N-substituent the product is a benzoxazine or an iminoisobenzofuran.
